plainEnglish: For pages that create legal or financial commitments, modify or delete user data, or submit test answers, provide a safety net: make submissions reversible, check inputs for errors, or let users review and confirm before finalizing. This prevents costly mistakes on high-stakes actions.
whyItMatters: Users with disabilities—and anyone—can make irreversible, costly mistakes (a wrong payment, a deleted account) when there's no review, confirmation, or undo step.
quickCheck: For checkout, account changes, or deletions, confirm there's a review/confirm step, input checking, or a way to reverse the action.
commonMistakes: Finalizing a payment or legal agreement with no review-and-confirm step.
Permanently deleting data with no confirmation or undo.
Not re-checking critical inputs before submission.

codeExamples.label: Charges immediately with no confirmation
codeExamples.code:
{/* No review step before an irreversible charge */}
<button onClick={placeOrder}>Buy now</button>
